---
title: Mid-semester Test Practice
output:
  moodlequiz::moodlequiz:
    replicates: 1
moodlequiz:
  category: mst-practice-alt2
---

# Multiple Choice

## Which statement about dynamic documents is most accurate?

Which statement about dynamic documents is most accurate?

`r cloze_singlechoice(choices(c("They combine analysis code and narrative text so outputs can be regenerated automatically.", "They are static reports that must be edited manually after each analysis update.", "They only work when exporting to PDF.", "They cannot include figures produced from code."), "They combine analysis code and narrative text so outputs can be regenerated automatically."), type = "vertical")`


## Why are absolute file paths usually discouraged in collaborative reproducible projects?

Why are absolute file paths usually discouraged in collaborative reproducible projects?

`r cloze_singlechoice(choices(c("They are slower than relative paths in all operating systems.", "They often depend on one person's machine-specific folder structure.", "They prevent use of Quarto chunk options.", "They are not supported by git."), "They often depend on one person's machine-specific folder structure."), type = "vertical")`


## Which Quarto chunk option prevents code from running?

Which Quarto chunk option prevents code from running?

`r cloze_singlechoice(choices(c("<code>echo: false</code>", "<code>message: false</code>", "<code>eval: false</code>", "<code>fig-cap:</code>"), "<code>eval: false</code>"), type = "vertical")`


## Which command best shows the current branch and whether local branches are ahead/behind their tracked remotes?

Which command best shows the current branch and whether local branches are ahead/behind their tracked remotes?

`r cloze_singlechoice(choices(c("<code>git branch -vv</code>", "<code>git remote add</code>", "<code>git stash list</code>", "<code>git log --graph</code>"), "<code>git branch -vv</code>"), type = "vertical")`


## Which command updates your local branch by fetching and then integrating remote changes in one step?

Which command updates your local branch by fetching and then integrating remote changes in one step?

`r cloze_singlechoice(choices(c("<code>git fetch</code>", "<code>git pull</code>", "<code>git clone</code>", "<code>git stash pop</code>"), "<code>git pull</code>"), type = "vertical")`


## You want to include only one stash entry (not necessarily the latest). Which command format is correct?

You want to include only one stash entry (not necessarily the latest). Which command format is correct?

`r cloze_singlechoice(choices(c("<code>git stash pop stash@&#123;1&#125;</code>", "<code>git stash merge stash@&#123;1&#125;</code>", "<code>git pop stash@&#123;1&#125;</code>", "<code>git apply stash@&#123;1&#125;</code>"), "<code>git stash pop stash@&#123;1&#125;</code>"), type = "vertical")`


## Which option best describes what a fork is on GitHub?

Which option best describes what a fork is on GitHub?

`r cloze_singlechoice(choices(c("A temporary local stash of uncommitted changes.", "A copy of another repository into your own GitHub account.", "A command that deletes old remote branches.", "A merge strategy used after rebase."), "A copy of another repository into your own GitHub account."), type = "vertical")`


## For section cross-references in Quarto (for example, @sec-intro), what is required?

For section cross-references in Quarto (for example, @sec-intro), what is required?

`r cloze_singlechoice(choices(c("The heading must include an identifier such as <code>sec-intro</code> and section numbering must be enabled.", "Only a figure label is needed.", "The section title must be written in ALL CAPS.", "A bibliography file must be present."), "The heading must include an identifier such as <code>sec-intro</code> and section numbering must be enabled."), type = "vertical")`


## Which statement about <code>git revert</code> is correct?

Which statement about <code>git revert</code> is correct?

`r cloze_singlechoice(choices(c("It removes commits from history permanently.", "It creates a new commit that undoes a previous commit.", "It only works before pushing to GitHub.", "It is identical to <code>git reset --hard</code>."), "It creates a new commit that undoes a previous commit."), type = "vertical")`


## You need to remove a remote branch called <code>draft-week7</code>. Which command is correct?

You need to remove a remote branch called <code>draft-week7</code>. Which command is correct?

`r cloze_singlechoice(choices(c("<code>git branch -d draft-week7</code>", "<code>git rm draft-week7</code>", "<code>git push origin --delete draft-week7</code>", "<code>git switch --delete draft-week7</code>"), "<code>git push origin --delete draft-week7</code>"), type = "vertical")`


# Free-answer questions

## Quarto YAML with multiple outputs {type=essay}

Write a minimal Quarto YAML header for a document titled "Practice Test 2" that:

* Includes your name as author,
* Renders to both HTML and PDF,
* Uses the HTML theme `cosmo`,
* Sets global execution so code is shown, warnings are hidden, and cache is enabled.


## Figure chunk and cross-reference {type=essay}

Write one R code chunk (including chunk options) that creates a scatter plot of `speed` vs `dist` from `cars`, and make it cross-referenceable.

Requirements:

* Label must begin with `fig-`,
* Add a caption via chunk options,
* Hide code but show the figure.

Then write one Markdown sentence that references your figure.


## Local vs remote synchronization {type=essay}

Briefly explain the difference between `git fetch` and `git pull`.

Then provide a short command sequence to:

* Check branch tracking status,
* Fetch updates from `origin`,
* Merge `origin/main` into local `main`.


## Using .gitignore effectively {type=essay}

Write a `.gitignore` snippet that ignores:

* All `.log` files,
* The `.Rhistory` and `.RData` files,
* Everything in `cache/`,
* Except for `cache/README.md`.


## Stash workflow scenario {type=essay}

You are halfway through edits on `report.qmd` and `analysis.R`, but must urgently switch to another branch to fix a bug.

Write a safe stash-based command sequence to:

* Save your current work with a message,
* Switch to `main`,
* Return to your feature branch later,
* Re-apply your saved work.

Add one short line describing the difference between using `apply` and `pop` at the final step.
